Entrance of the stone chamber | Other Names = Ishi no Karabitsu | Location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | Coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | Map = Japan Hyogo | Label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square burial mound (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place Names II|series=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} [[Category:Kofun in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | Name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other Names = Ishi no Karabitsu | Location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | Coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | Map = Japan Hyogo | Label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square burial mound (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger burial mounds in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other burial mounds in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place Names II|series=Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} [[Category:Kofun in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | Coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | Map = Japan Hyogo | Label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square burial mound (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger burial mounds in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other burial mounds in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} [[Category:Kofun in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]]

While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} [[Category:Kofun in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | Coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | Map = Japan Hyogo | Label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square burial mound (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger burial mounds in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other burial mounds in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} [[Category:Kofun in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]]

The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger burial mounds in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other burial mounds in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} [[Category:Kofun in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | Coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | Map = Japan Hyogo | Label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square burial mound (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} [[Category:Kofun in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]][[category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | Coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | Map = Japan Hyogo | Label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square burial mound (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger burial mounds in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other burial mounds in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | Coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | Map = Japan Hyogo | Label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square burial mound (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger burial mounds in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other burial mounds in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | Coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | Map = Japan Hyogo | Label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square burial mound (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger burial mounds in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other burial mounds in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | Coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | Map = Japan Hyogo | Label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square burial mound (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger burial mounds in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other burial mounds in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | Coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | Map = Japan Hyogo | Label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square burial mound (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger burial mounds in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other burial mounds in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Takarazuka]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | Coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | Map = Japan Hyogo | Label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square burial mound (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger burial mounds in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other burial mounds in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | Coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | Map = Japan Hyogo | Label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square burial mound (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger burial mounds in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other burial mounds in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Takarazuka]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | Coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | Map = Japan Hyogo | Label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square burial mound (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger burial mounds in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other burial mounds in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal burial mound, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite and Yamamoto Burial Mounds|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun
