revision.comment: doing more mass replacements to fix the issues
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-scale excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | Shape = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label Position = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original shape is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a carved {{ill|house-typed sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-typed Sarcophagus}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-typed stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-typed sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == other_designation == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture other_designation]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == other_designation == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture other_designation]}} Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == other_designation == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == other_designation == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture other_designation]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_designation ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_designation. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == other_designation == === Hyogo Prefecture other_designations === * other_designation ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture other_designation]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_designation ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_designation. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == other_designation == === Hyogo Prefecture other_designations === * other_designation ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture other_designation]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_designation. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == other_designation == === Hyogo Prefecture other_designations === * other_designation ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture other_designation]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_other_designation ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_other_designation. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == other_other_designation == === Hyogo Prefecture other_other_designations === * other_other_designation ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture other_other_designation]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_other_designation ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_other_designation. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == other_other_designation == === Hyogo Prefecture other_other_designations === * other_other_designation ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture other_other_designation]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_designation ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_designation. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == other_designation == === Hyogo Prefecture other_designations === * other_designation ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture other_designation]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_designation ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_designation. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == other_designation == === Hyogo Prefecture other_designations === * other_designation ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture other_designation]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_designation. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == other_designation == === Hyogo Prefecture other_designations === * other_designation ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture other_designation]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} | Period = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_designation ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture other_designation. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == other_designation == === Hyogo Prefecture other_designations === * other_designation ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture other_designation]}}
