Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] [[sn:hiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 白鳥塚古墳 / 中山寺古墳 |画像 = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
石室開口部 |別名 = 石の唐櫃 |所属 = |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[宝塚市]][[中山寺 (宝塚市)|中山寺]]2丁目
（[[中山寺 (宝塚市)|中山寺]]境内） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 49|緯度秒 = 16.40 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 22|経度秒 = 3.63 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = 不明 |規模 = |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に刳抜式[[家形石棺]]） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末-[[7世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 兵庫県指定史跡「中山寺古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Hyogo |アイコン = |ラベル = 白鳥塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = left }} '''白鳥塚古墳'''（はくちょうづかこふん）または'''中山寺古墳'''（なかやまでらこふん）は、[[兵庫県]][[宝塚市]][[中山寺 (宝塚市)|中山寺]]にある[[古墳]]。兵庫県指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「中山寺古墳」）。 == 概要 == 兵庫県東部、長尾山丘陵の南麓に築造された古墳である。現在は[[中山寺 (宝塚市)|中山寺]]境内に所在する。墳丘は大きく改変を受けているほか、石室実測調査が実施されているが発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳丘の元の形状は改変のため明らかでない（一説に一辺約25メートル以上の[[方墳]]）{{Sfn|兵庫県の地名|1999}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。石室現存長16.2メートルを測る大型石室で、石材には[[花崗岩]]の巨石を使用する{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。石室の玄室内には加古川流域産の竜山石製竜山石は、かつて流紋岩質溶結凝灰岩とされてきたが、近年の調査によって流紋岩質成層ハイアロクラスタイト（水冷破砕岩）と判明している（[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html 宝殿石（竜山石）]（兵庫県ホームページ）参照）。の刳抜式家形石棺を据える{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期-[[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[6世紀]]末-[[7世紀]]初頭{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}（TK209型式期{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}）頃と推定される{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。長尾山丘陵では丘陵東半部で[[群集墳]]の営造が知られるが、本古墳はそれらとはやや離れた位置に築造される。本古墳の石室は長尾山丘陵では最大規模で大和の大型石室にも匹敵する規模であり、石室の構造も長尾山丘陵の石室よりは大和の石室に共通性が認められる点で特色を示す{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。特異な[[八角墳]]として知られる[[中山荘園古墳]]（宝塚市中山荘園）と合わせて、西摂平野に勢力を有した大豪族の存在を示唆する点で重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|兵庫県の地名|1999}}。なお被葬者は明らかでないが、中山寺の寺伝では第14代[[仲哀天皇]]の先皇后の大中姫とする。 古墳域は[[1960年]]（[[昭和]]35年）に兵庫県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[江戸時代]]の『[[摂津名所図会]]』に「白鳥窟 俗に石唐櫃と云う。忍熊王廟なり」の記載{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 * [[1887年]]（[[明治]]20年）、[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]による調査{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 * [[1960年]]（[[昭和]]35年）3月31日、兵庫県指定史跡に指定。 * [[2015年]]度（[[平成]]27年度）、石室の三次元計測（ゴーランド・コレクション調査プロジェクト、[[2017年]]に報告）{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 * 石室全長：現存16.2メートル * 玄室：長さ5.5メートル、幅2.4メートル（中央付近）、高さ2.9メートル（奥壁付近） * 羨道：長さ10.6メートル、幅1.8メートル（玄門付近）、高さ2.0メートル（玄門付近） 石室の石材には主に[[花崗岩]]の巨石が使用され、一部に結晶質[[凝灰岩]]が用いられる{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。玄室の奥壁は3段積みで、側壁は2-3段積み{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。玄室の天井石は2枚{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。後世の改変として、玄門に石枠・鉄扉が設置されているほか、羨道の開口部には小型石材が追加され、玄室・羨道の床面に敷石やその境に段が付加されている{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 石室の玄室内の中央北寄りには加古川流域産の竜山石製の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]1基を据える。石棺の規模は次の通り。 * 棺身：長さ1.80メートル、幅1.06メートル、高さ0.66メートル * 蓋石：長さ1.90メートル、幅1.16メートル、高さ0.49メートル 棺身は原位置を保つ可能性が高いとされる{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。蓋石には前後1対・左右2対の縄掛突起計6個を付す{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。蓋石の上部平坦面指数（上部平坦面幅の全体幅に対する割合）は43{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), sekkan.jpg|玄室の家形石棺 Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） == 文化財 == === 兵庫県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 中山寺古墳 - 1960年（昭和35年）3月31日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf 県指定文化財一覧]}}（兵庫県立教育研修所）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（兵庫県教育委員会、1974年設置） * 調査報告書 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 兵庫県宝塚市 白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群 -ゴーランド調査古墳の研究1-]|series=ゴーランド・コレクション調査研究報告書第1号|publisher=ゴーランド・コレクション調査プロジェクト|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=福井英治|year=1989|chapter=白鳥塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=中山寺古墳|title= 兵庫県の地名|volume=II|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]29-2|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490611|ref={{Harvid|兵庫県の地名|1999}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=直宮憲一|year=1999|chapter=中山寺白鳥塚古墳について|title=中山寺の歴史と文化財|publisher=大本山中山寺|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Hakuchozuka Kofun}} * [[中山寺 (宝塚市)]] * [[中山荘園古墳]] * [[北米谷古墓]] * [[鉢塚古墳 revision.comment: remarking some of the new pages
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|12=draft|12=draft}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド|12=draft|12=draft}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] [[sn:hiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]]

Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Hakuchozuka Kofun Takarazuka}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]] [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] [[sn:hiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|12=draft|12=draft}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド|12=draft|12=draft}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド|12=draft|12=draft}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} {{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|12=draft|12=draft}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド|12=draft|12=draft}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{draft revision.comment: 32 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市) up to rev 104577584
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 白鳥塚古墳 / 中山寺古墳 |画像 = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
石室開口部 |別名 = 石の唐櫃 |所属 = |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[宝塚市]][[中山寺 (宝塚市)|中山寺]]2丁目
（[[中山寺 (宝塚市)|中山寺]]境内） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 49|緯度秒 = 16.40 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 22|経度秒 = 3.63 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = 不明 |規模 = |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に刳抜式[[家形石棺]]） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末-[[7世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 兵庫県指定史跡「中山寺古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Hyogo |アイコン = |ラベル = 白鳥塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = left }} '''白鳥塚古墳'''（はくちょうづかこふん）または'''中山寺古墳'''（なかやまでらこふん）は、[[兵庫県]][[宝塚市]][[中山寺 (宝塚市)|中山寺]]にある[[古墳]]。兵庫県指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「中山寺古墳」）。 == 概要 == 兵庫県東部、長尾山丘陵の南麓に築造された古墳である。現在は[[中山寺 (宝塚市)|中山寺]]境内に所在する。墳丘は大きく改変を受けているほか、石室実測調査が実施されているが発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳丘の元の形状は改変のため明らかでない（一説に一辺約25メートル以上の[[方墳]]）{{Sfn|兵庫県の地名|1999}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。石室現存長16.2メートルを測る大型石室で、石材には[[花崗岩]]の巨石を使用する{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。石室の玄室内には加古川流域産の竜山石製竜山石は、かつて流紋岩質溶結凝灰岩とされてきたが、近年の調査によって流紋岩質成層ハイアロクラスタイト（水冷破砕岩）と判明している（[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html 宝殿石（竜山石）]（兵庫県ホームページ）参照）。の刳抜式家形石棺を据える{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期-[[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[6世紀]]末-[[7世紀]]初頭{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}（TK209型式期{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}）頃と推定される{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。長尾山丘陵では丘陵東半部で[[群集墳]]の営造が知られるが、本古墳はそれらとはやや離れた位置に築造される。本古墳の石室は長尾山丘陵では最大規模で大和の大型石室にも匹敵する規模であり、石室の構造も長尾山丘陵の石室よりは大和の石室に共通性が認められる点で特色を示す{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。特異な[[八角墳]]として知られる[[中山荘園古墳]]（宝塚市中山荘園）と合わせて、西摂平野に勢力を有した大豪族の存在を示唆する点で重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|兵庫県の地名|1999}}。なお被葬者は明らかでないが、中山寺の寺伝では第14代[[仲哀天皇]]の先皇后の大中姫とする。 古墳域は[[1960年]]（[[昭和]]35年）に兵庫県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[江戸時代]]の『[[摂津名所図会]]』に「白鳥窟 俗に石唐櫃と云う。忍熊王廟なり」の記載{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 * [[1887年]]（[[明治]]20年）、[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]による調査{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 * [[1960年]]（[[昭和]]35年）3月31日、兵庫県指定史跡に指定。 * [[2015年]]度（[[平成]]27年度）、石室の三次元計測（ゴーランド・コレクション調査プロジェクト、[[2017年]]に報告）{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 * 石室全長：現存16.2メートル * 玄室：長さ5.5メートル、幅2.4メートル（中央付近）、高さ2.9メートル（奥壁付近） * 羨道：長さ10.6メートル、幅1.8メートル（玄門付近）、高さ2.0メートル（玄門付近） 石室の石材には主に[[花崗岩]]の巨石が使用され、一部に結晶質[[凝灰岩]]が用いられる{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。玄室の奥壁は3段積みで、側壁は2-3段積み{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。玄室の天井石は2枚{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。後世の改変として、玄門に石枠・鉄扉が設置されているほか、羨道の開口部には小型石材が追加され、玄室・羨道の床面に敷石やその境に段が付加されている{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 石室の玄室内の中央北寄りには加古川流域産の竜山石製の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]1基を据える。石棺の規模は次の通り。 * 棺身：長さ1.80メートル、幅1.06メートル、高さ0.66メートル * 蓋石：長さ1.90メートル、幅1.16メートル、高さ0.49メートル 棺身は原位置を保つ可能性が高いとされる{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。蓋石には前後1対・左右2対の縄掛突起計6個を付す{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。蓋石の上部平坦面指数（上部平坦面幅の全体幅に対する割合）は43{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), sekkan.jpg|玄室の家形石棺 Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） == 文化財 == === 兵庫県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 中山寺古墳 - 1960年（昭和35年）3月31日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf 県指定文化財一覧]}}（兵庫県立教育研修所）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == -ゴーランド調査古墳の研究1-]|series=ゴーランド・コレクション調査研究報告書第1号|publisher=ゴーランド・コレクション調査プロジェクト|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=福井英治|year=1989|chapter=白鳥塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=中山寺古墳|title= 兵庫県の地名|volume=II|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]29-2|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490611|ref={{Harvid|兵庫県の地名|1999}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=直宮憲一|year=1999|chapter=中山寺白鳥塚古墳について|title=中山寺の歴史と文化財|publisher=大本山中山寺|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Hakuchozuka Kofun}} * [[中山寺 (宝塚市)]] * [[中山荘園古墳]] * [[北米谷古墓]] * [[鉢塚古墳 (池田市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:はくちようつかこふん たからつかし}} [[Category:兵庫県の古墳]] [[Category:宝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:兵庫県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] to [[Shiratorizuka Kofun (Takarazuka)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 白鳥塚古墳 / 中山寺古墳 |画像 = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
石室開口部 |別名 = 石の唐櫃 |所属 = |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[宝塚市]][[中山寺 (宝塚市)|中山寺]]2丁目
（[[中山寺 (宝塚市)|中山寺]]境内） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 49|緯度秒 = 16.40 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 22|経度秒 = 3.63 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = 不明 |規模 = |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に刳抜式[[家形石棺]]） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末-[[7世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 兵庫県指定史跡「中山寺古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Hyogo |アイコン = |ラベル = 白鳥塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = left }} '''白鳥塚古墳'''（はくちょうづかこふん）または'''中山寺古墳'''（なかやまでらこふん）は、[[兵庫県]][[宝塚市]][[中山寺 (宝塚市)|中山寺]]にある[[古墳]]。兵庫県指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「中山寺古墳」）。 == 概要 == 兵庫県東部、長尾山丘陵の南麓に築造された古墳である。現在は[[中山寺 (宝塚市)|中山寺]]境内に所在する。墳丘は大きく改変を受けているほか、石室実測調査が実施されているが発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳丘の元の形状は改変のため明らかでない（一説に一辺約25メートル以上の[[方墳]]）{{Sfn|兵庫県の地名|1999}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。石室現存長16.2メートルを測る大型石室で、石材には[[花崗岩]]の巨石を使用する{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。石室の玄室内には加古川流域産の竜山石製竜山石は、かつて流紋岩質溶結凝灰岩とされてきたが、近年の調査によって流紋岩質成層ハイアロクラスタイト（水冷破砕岩）と判明している（[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html 宝殿石（竜山石）]（兵庫県ホームページ）参照）。の刳抜式家形石棺を据える{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期-[[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[6世紀]]末-[[7世紀]]初頭{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}（TK209型式期{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}）頃と推定される{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。長尾山丘陵では丘陵東半部で[[群集墳]]の営造が知られるが、本古墳はそれらとはやや離れた位置に築造される。本古墳の石室は長尾山丘陵では最大規模で大和の大型石室にも匹敵する規模であり、石室の構造も長尾山丘陵の石室よりは大和の石室に共通性が認められる点で特色を示す{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。特異な[[八角墳]]として知られる[[中山荘園古墳]]（宝塚市中山荘園）と合わせて、西摂平野に勢力を有した大豪族の存在を示唆する点で重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|兵庫県の地名|1999}}。なお被葬者は明らかでないが、中山寺の寺伝では第14代[[仲哀天皇]]の先皇后の大中姫とする。 古墳域は[[1960年]]（[[昭和]]35年）に兵庫県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[江戸時代]]の『[[摂津名所図会]]』に「白鳥窟 俗に石唐櫃と云う。忍熊王廟なり」の記載{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 * [[1887年]]（[[明治]]20年）、[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]による調査{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 * [[1960年]]（[[昭和]]35年）3月31日、兵庫県指定史跡に指定。 * [[2015年]]度（[[平成]]27年度）、石室の三次元計測（ゴーランド・コレクション調査プロジェクト、[[2017年]]に報告）{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 * 石室全長：現存16.2メートル * 玄室：長さ5.5メートル、幅2.4メートル（中央付近）、高さ2.9メートル（奥壁付近） * 羨道：長さ10.6メートル、幅1.8メートル（玄門付近）、高さ2.0メートル（玄門付近） 石室の石材には主に[[花崗岩]]の巨石が使用され、一部に結晶質[[凝灰岩]]が用いられる{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。玄室の奥壁は3段積みで、側壁は2-3段積み{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。玄室の天井石は2枚{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。後世の改変として、玄門に石枠・鉄扉が設置されているほか、羨道の開口部には小型石材が追加され、玄室・羨道の床面に敷石やその境に段が付加されている{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 石室の玄室内の中央北寄りには加古川流域産の竜山石製の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]1基を据える。石棺の規模は次の通り。 * 棺身：長さ1.80メートル、幅1.06メートル、高さ0.66メートル * 蓋石：長さ1.90メートル、幅1.16メートル、高さ0.49メートル 棺身は原位置を保つ可能性が高いとされる{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。蓋石には前後1対・左右2対の縄掛突起計6個を付す{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。蓋石の上部平坦面指数（上部平坦面幅の全体幅に対する割合）は43{{Sfn|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}。 Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), sekkan.jpg|玄室の家形石棺 Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） == 文化財 == === 兵庫県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 中山寺古墳 - 1960年（昭和35年）3月31日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf 県指定文化財一覧]}}（兵庫県立教育研修所）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == -ゴーランド調査古墳の研究1-]|series=ゴーランド・コレクション調査研究報告書第1号|publisher=ゴーランド・コレクション調査プロジェクト|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|白鳥塚古墳・山本古墳群|2017}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=福井英治|year=1989|chapter=白鳥塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=中山寺古墳|title= 兵庫県の地名|volume=II|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]29-2|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490611|ref={{Harvid|兵庫県の地名|1999}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite (池田市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:はくちようつかこふん たからつかし}} [[Category:兵庫県の古墳]] [[Category:宝塚市の歴史]] [[Category:兵庫県指定史跡]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyogo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyogo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyogo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyogo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = 34°49′16.40″N 135°22′3.63″E | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyogo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-dera|qq=}} precincts in [[Takarazuka City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyōgo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/William Gowland|qq=}}. * [[1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ''' Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyogo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyogo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|ja|中山寺 (宝塚市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama-dera|qq=}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|ja|中山荘園古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayamasōen Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)|version=104577584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]
