While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by [[:en:William Gowland|William Gowland]]. * [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[:en:2015|2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ** Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyōgo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Nakayama-dera|Nakayama-dera]] * [[:en:Nakayamasōen Kofun|Nakayamasōen Kofun]] * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)|version=104577584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] {{Commons category|Hakuchozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11580553}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[commons:Category:Hakuchozuka Kofun]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] [[commons:Category:Hakuchozuka Kofun]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Hakuchozuka Kofun]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580553]] Wikidata name: Hakuchozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[:en:Takarazuka City|Takarazuka City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = {{Coord|34.821222|N|135.367675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyōgo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Nakayama-dera|Nakayama-dera]] precincts in [[:en:Takarazuka City|Takarazuka City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyōgo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by [[:en:William Gowland|William Gowland]]. * [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[:en:2015|2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ** Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyōgo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Nakayama-dera|Nakayama-dera]] * [[:en:Nakayamasōen Kofun|Nakayamasōen Kofun]] * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)|version=104577584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] {{Commons category|Hakuchozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11580553}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = {{Coord|34.821222|N|135.367675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyōgo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Nakayama-dera|Nakayama-dera]] precincts in [[:en:Takarazuka City|Takarazuka City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyōgo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by [[:en:William Gowland|William Gowland]]. * [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[:en:2015|2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ** Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyōgo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Nakayama-dera|Nakayama-dera]] * [[:en:Nakayamasōen Kofun|Nakayamasōen Kofun]] * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)|version=104577584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] {{Commons category|Hakuchozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11580553}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ** Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyōgo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Nakayama-dera|Nakayama-dera]] * [[:en:Nakayamasōen Kofun|Nakayamasōen Kofun]] * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)|version=104577584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] {{Commons category|Hakuchozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11580553}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[co:commons:Category:Hakuchozuka Kofun]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Hakuchozuka Kofun]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Hakuchozuka Kofun]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580553]] Wikidata name: Hakuchozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[:en:Takarazuka City|Takarazuka City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = {{Coord|34.821222|N|135.367675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyōgo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Nakayama-dera|Nakayama-dera]] precincts in [[:en:Takarazuka City|Takarazuka City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyōgo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by [[:en:William Gowland|William Gowland]]. * [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site. * [[:en:2015|2015]]: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ** Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyōgo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Nakayama-dera|Nakayama-dera]] * [[:en:Nakayamasōen Kofun|Nakayamasōen Kofun]] * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)|version=104577584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] {{Commons category|Hakuchozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11580553}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Qq]]

Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = {{Coord|34.821222|N|135.367675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyōgo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|en|Nakayama-dera|WD=Q3335345|lt=Nakayama-dera}} precincts in [[:en:Takarazuka City|Takarazuka City]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyōgo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * {{ill|1887|en|1887|WD=Q7826|lt=1887}} (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|en|William Gowland|WD=Q3568666|lt=William Gowland}}. * {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960}} (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site. * {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015}}: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ** Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyōgo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|en|Nakayama-dera|WD=Q3335345|lt=Nakayama-dera}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|en|Nakayamasōen Kofun|WD=Q11364274|lt=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)|version=104577584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] {{Commons category|Hakuchozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11580553}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * {{ill|1887|en|1887|WD=Q7826|lt=1887}} (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|en|William Gowland|WD=Q3568666|lt=William Gowland}}. * {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960}} (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site. * {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015}}: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ** Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyōgo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|en|Nakayama-dera|WD=Q3335345|lt=Nakayama-dera}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|en|Nakayamasōen Kofun|WD=Q11364274|lt=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)|version=104577584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] {{Commons category|Hakuchozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11580553}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Hakuchozuka Kofun]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Hakuchozuka Kofun]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Hakuchozuka Kofun]][[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hakuchozuka Kofun / Nakayama-dera Kofun | image = [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance of the stone chamber | Other names = Ishi no Karabitsu | location = [[Nakayama-dera (Takarazuka)|Nakayama-dera]], 2-chome, [[:en:Takarazuka City|Takarazuka City]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}
Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = {{Coord|34.821222|N|135.367675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyōgo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|en|Nakayama-dera|WD=Q3335345|lt=Nakayama-dera}} precincts in [[:en:Takarazuka City|Takarazuka City]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyōgo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * {{ill|1887|en|1887|WD=Q7826|lt=1887}} (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|en|William Gowland|WD=Q3568666|lt=William Gowland}}. * {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960}} (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site. * {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015}}: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ** Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyōgo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|en|Nakayama-dera|WD=Q3335345|lt=Nakayama-dera}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|en|Nakayamasōen Kofun|WD=Q11364274|lt=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)|version=104577584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] {{Commons category|Hakuchozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11580553}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyōgo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * {{ill|1887|en|1887|WD=Q7826|lt=1887|1=1887}} (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|en|William Gowland|WD=Q3568666|lt=William Gowland|1=William Gowland}}. * {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960|1=1960}} (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site. * {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}}: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ** Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyōgo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|en|Nakayama-dera|WD=Q3335345|lt=Nakayama-dera|1=Nakayama-dera}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|en|Nakayamasōen Kofun|WD=Q11364274|lt=Nakayamasōen Kofun|1=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)|version=104577584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] {{Commons category|Hakuchozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11580553}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera | coordinates = {{Coord|34.821222|N|135.367675|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a carved {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} | built = Late [[Kofun period]], late 6th century - early 7th century | Status = Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site ("Nakayama-dera Kofun") | map = Japan Hyōgo | map_dot_label = Left }} '''Hakuchozuka Kofun''' (白鳥塚古墳), also known as '''Nakayama-dera Kofun''' (中山寺古墳), is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Nakayama-dera|en|Nakayama-dera|WD=Q3335345|lt=Nakayama-dera|1=Nakayama-dera}} precincts in [[:en:Takarazuka City|Takarazuka City]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hyōgo Prefecture under the name "Nakayama-dera Kofun." == Overview == The Hakuchozuka Kofun was constructed on the southern foothills of the Nagao Hills in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. Currently located within the precincts of Nakayama-dera, the original mound has been significantly altered, and while the stone chamber has been surveyed, no full-map_size excavation has been conducted. The mound's original type is unknown due to modifications, but it is hypothesized to have been a square Kofun (''hōfun'') with sides over 25 meters{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast, measuring a total length of 16.2 meters. The chamber uses massive granite stones and features a carved house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The construction period is estimated to be between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, corresponding to the end of the [[Kofun period]] and the start of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. The stone chamber’s size and construction closely resemble those of larger Kofuns in the Yamato region, distinguishing it from other Kofuns in the Nagao Hills{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == Cultural Significance == The mound is significant for its structural and cultural connections to elite burial practices. The presence of the Nakayama-soen Kofun nearby, a rare octagonal Kofun, further emphasizes the importance of this region as the base of a powerful ruling clan in the Nishisetsu Plain{{Sfn|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}. While the identity of the buried individual is unknown, local traditions associate it with Princess Onaka, consort of Emperor Chūai. == Excavation History == * {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}: Documented in the ''Settsu Meisho Zue,'' describing it as the mausoleum of Prince Oshikuma. * {{ill|1887|en|1887|WD=Q7826|lt=1887|1=1887}} (Meiji 20): Survey conducted by {{ill|William Gowland|en|William Gowland|WD=Q3568666|lt=William Gowland|1=William Gowland}}. * {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960|1=1960}} (Showa 35): Designated as a Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site. * {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}}: A 3D survey of the chamber conducted as part of the Gowland Collection Survey Project{{Sfn|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}. == burial == [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-B-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Hakuchozuka Kofun (Takarazuka), 3D-N-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan of the stone chamber}}]] The chamber measures: * Total length: 16.2 meters * Main chamber: Length 5.5 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 2.9 meters * Corridor: Length 10.6 meters, width 1.8 meters, height 2.0 meters Inside, a house-shaped sarcophagus made of Ryusanyama stone is placed in the northern section of the main chamber. The sarcophagus measures: * Base: Length 1.80 m, width 1.06 m, height 0.66 m * Lid: Length 1.90 m, width 1.16 m, height 0.49 m == Historic Site == === Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Sites === * Historic Site ** Nakayama-dera Kofun - Designated on March 31, 1960{{PDFlink|[http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf List of Hyōgo Prefecture Historic Site]}} (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education).. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description panel at the site (Hyōgo Prefectural Board of Education, 1974) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2017|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/62746 Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns]|publisher=Gowland Collection Survey Project|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun and Yamamoto Kofuns|2017}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Fukui Eiji|year=1989|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Hakuchozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1999|title=Hyōgo Place names II|series=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia Vol. 29-2|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Nakayama-dera Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nakayama-dera|en|Nakayama-dera|WD=Q3335345|lt=Nakayama-dera|1=Nakayama-dera}} * {{ill|Nakayamasōen Kofun|en|Nakayamasōen Kofun|WD=Q11364274|lt=Nakayamasōen Kofun|1=Nakayamasōen Kofun}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)|version=104577584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:白鳥塚古墳 (宝塚市)]] {{Commons category|Hakuchozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11580553}} [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
